en.extract:
The Papuan treecreeper (Cormobates placens) is a species of bird in the family Climacteridae. It was previously considered a subspecies of the white-throated treecreeper (C. leucophaea).
It is found in the highlands of New Guinea.

The '''Papuan treecreeper''' ('''''Cormobates placens''''') is a [[species]] of [[bird]] in the family [[Climacteridae]].<ref>[http://www.hbw.com/species/papuan-treecreeper-cormobates-placens "Papuan Treecreeper (Cormobates placens)"] ''Handbook of the Birds of the World''. Retrieved 2014-3-23.</ref> It was previously considered a subspecies of the [[white-throated treecreeper]] (''C. leucophaea'').<ref>{{cite book |title=Systematics and Taxonomy of Australian Birds|vauthors=Christidis L, Boles WE |year=2008 |publisher=CSIRO Publishing |location=Canberra |isbn=978-0-643-06511-6 |page=179}}</ref>
 
It is found in the highlands of [[New Guinea]].
